title: EML-4 Formal Proof III: Primitive Count & Asymmetry Equivalence

log.produces: EML-2 from EML-1 (or EML-2 from EML-0 as log∘log needs 2 steps)
log.example: log(x): EML-0→EML-2 directly (log is depth-2 primitive)
log.note: log is the EML-2 primitive; depth contribution depends on composition

integration.example: ∫f dμ: equivalent to log primitive by S219
integration.note: ∫ = equivalent to log at depth 2 (Δd=2 measure theorem)

oscillation.note: Oscillation (imaginary exp) is the EML-3 primitive

no_fourth_finite_primitive.what_would_be_needed: A depth-increasing operator beyond oscillation that stays finite
no_fourth_finite_primitive.conclusion: EML has exactly 3 finite depth-increasing primitives: exp, log, oscillation

3_primitives_all.reason: exp(log(exp(ix))) = exp(ix) = EML-3; no new depth gained
3_primitives_all.conclusion: IMPOSSIBLE to exceed Δd=2 with finite primitives

primitive_count_theorem.theorem: Δd ≤ 2 for any finite combination of EML primitives
primitive_count_theorem.corollary: Δd=3 is impossible; Δd=∞ requires Horizon crossing (non-primitive)

asymmetry_equivalence.step_1: Δd=3 definition → requires landing at EML-4
asymmetry_equivalence.step_3: EML primitives: exp, log, osc — but osc=exp(i·) → 2 independent
asymmetry_equivalence.equivalence: EML-4 gap ↔ Δd=3 prohibition ↔ Primitive Count ≤ 2

primitive_analysis.key_insight: EML has 2 independent finite primitives; Δd ≤ 2; EML-4 inaccessible

eml_depth_summary.equivalences: EML-4 gap ↔ Δd=3 forbidden ↔ Primitive Count ≤ 2

key_theorem: The EML Primitive Count Theorem (S223, Direction A): The EML operator eml(x,y) = exp(x) - ln(y) has exactly 3 finite primitives: exp (depth 1), log/∫ (depth 2), oscillation exp(i·) (depth 3). However, oscillation = exp(imaginary) combines exp with the imaginary unit — it is NOT independent of exp. The TRUE independent count = 2: {exp, log}. Maximum Δd from k independent finite primitives = k. With 2 independent primitives: max Δd = 2. Δd=3 is impossible without EML-4. This proves: EML-4 gap ↔ Δd=3 prohibition ↔ Primitive Independence Count ≤ 2. All three are equivalent statements about the same structural constraint.
rabbit_hole_log: Oscillation = exp(i·): not independent of exp — reduces primitive count to 2
Primitive count theorem: Δd = number of independent primitives (max 2 finite)
Three-way equivalence: EML-4 gap ↔ Δd=3 prohibition ↔ primitive independence ≤ 2
